Notes: Baseline model using economic development and relative healthcare spending.
Tests diminishing returns of income by applying a logarithmic transformation to GDP per capita.
Non-linear baseline model using the same variables as M1.
Adds education as a social factor; sample size decreases substantially due to missing data.
Adds obesity as the first lifestyle variable.
Adds obesity and smoking; smoking improves predictive performance.
Replaces health expenditure as % of GDP with health expenditure per capita.
Adds PM2.5 air pollution using 2020 data as the most recent available environmental proxy.
Final candidate model combining economic development, absolute healthcare spending, lifestyle factors and air pollution.
